title: Study Configuration
description: Configuration schema for an encoding study in web image formats research. A study defines a targeted experiment where specific encoding parameters are swept across a set of source images.

$schema.description: Reference to the JSON schema for IDE validation

id.description: Unique identifier for the study (used in file paths and CLI)

description.description: Brief description of the study's purpose and what it investigates

time_budget.description: Maximum wall-clock time in seconds for the pipeline. When set, the pipeline processes as many images as possible within this budget. Can be overridden via CLI.

comparison.description: Configuration for visual comparison figure generation. Controls which parameter varies within each comparison figure (tiles) and which splits across figures.

tile_parameter.description: The encoding parameter that should vary within each comparison figure (i.e. each tile shows a different value of this parameter). All other varying parameters become split axes that produce separate figures. For multi-format studies use 'format'; for speed/effort/method sweeps use the sweep parameter so tiles show all its values at a fixed quality level.

targets.description: Target metric values for comparison figure generation. Each entry specifies a metric and a list of target values at which comparison figures should be produced. For quality metrics (ssimulacra2, psnr, ssim, butteraugli), the comparison script interpolates encoder quality settings to match each target. For bytes_per_pixel, it interpolates to match target file size ratios.

values.description: Target values for this metric. Each value produces a separate set of comparison figures.

exclude_images.description: List of image filenames to exclude from automatic source-image selection for comparison figures. Use this to skip images where synthetic quality metrics do not reflect perceived visual quality well. Filenames are matched against the basename of source/original image paths (e.g. '0801.png').

analysis.description: Configuration for analysis plot generation. Overrides the automatic parameter detection heuristics.

x_axis.description: The parameter to use as the primary x-axis in quality metric and efficiency plots. When omitted, the parameter with the most unique values is used.

group_by.description: The parameter to use for line grouping in plots (each unique value becomes a separate line). When omitted, the parameter with the second-most unique values is used.

id.description: Dataset identifier matching an entry in datasets.json

max_images.description: Maximum number of images to use from the dataset. Acts as an upper bound even when time_budget is set. If omitted, all images are used.

encoders.description: List of encoder configurations to sweep. Each entry defines one encoder and the parameter space to explore.

quality.description: Quality settings to sweep. Can be a single value, an explicit list, or a range definition.

oneOf.description: Range definition for generating quality values

chroma_subsampling.description: Chroma subsampling modes to sweep. Only applicable to formats that support it (AVIF, JPEG XL). If omitted, encoder default is used.

speed.description: AVIF encoder speed settings to sweep (0-10, where 0=slowest/best quality, 10=fastest). Only applicable to AVIF format.

effort.description: JPEG XL encoder effort settings to sweep (1-10, where higher=slower/better quality). Only applicable to JPEG XL format.

method.description: WebP compression method settings to sweep (0-6, where 0=fast, 6=slowest/best quality). Only applicable to WebP format.

resolution.description: Target resolutions (longest edge in pixels) to sweep. Each value produces a downscaled copy of the source image before encoding. If omitted, the original resolution is used.

extra_args.description: Encoder-specific extra arguments as key-value pairs. Keys are CLI argument names (without leading dashes), values are argument values. These are passed directly to the encoder CLI tool.
